does: PREFINE extends Direct Preference Optimization from language models to continuous control by fine-tuning pre-trained RL policies with trajectory-level safety preferences. Given a reward-optimized policy and a small labeled dataset of preferred low-cost versus dispreferred high-cost trajectories, the method jointly optimizes an implicit reward model and cost model without numerical cost values. It constructs policy-sampled counterfactual trajectories to create meaningful preference contrasts, enabling the policy to generate lower-cost behaviors while preserving high rewards through a single fine-tuning pass rather than retraining from scratch.
connects: This work builds on Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which has become standard for LLM alignment by learning from pairwise preferences without explicit reward modeling. It extends preference-based learning from single-step language model completions to trajectory-level decisions in sequential control, addressing a key gap where standard RLHF assumptions break down. The paper responds to the practical challenge of safety alignment in RL without numerical cost specifications, connecting to broader work on cost-constrained RL, multi-objective policy optimization, and safe reinforcement learning that seeks to incorporate safety constraints post-hoc into existing agents.
enables: PREFINE enables efficient safety fine-tuning of deployed RL systems by leveraging preference feedback rather than requiring explicit cost functions—useful when safety objectives are difficult to quantify numerically. The approach opens pathways for iterative safety improvements through human preference labels, aligning with broader trends toward preference-based alignment in both language and control domains. It suggests potential extensions to multi-modal preference aggregation, scaling preference learning to larger policy classes, and integrating safety constraints during online deployment where preference data could be collected from human demonstrations or safety audits.
